title: China Economy - South China Morning Post

items.title: As China dominates solar and energy sector, fears emerge over EU’s ‘high-risk’ crackdown

items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: As China dominates solar and energy sector, fears emerge over EU’s ‘high-risk’ crackdown
items.content:
“If you look around, you see China everywhere,” said the communications chief at a leading German home-storage maker, whose stand at Europe’s largest solar and energy storage exhibition in Munich was flanked by Chinese exhibitors including Fox ESS, HyperStrong and CATL.
The numbers backed him up. Of the more than 2,650 exhibitors at The Smarter E Europe last week, about 750 were registered in China, the largest contingent after host Germany. The true figure is higher still, as the list counts...

items.title: Alternate route: China invests in Europe cargo corridor that avoids Russia, Middle East
items.creator: Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Alternate route: China invests in Europe cargo corridor that avoids Russia, Middle East
items.content:
China is investing in a Caspian Sea import-export shipping route to Europe that avoids Russia and sidesteps conflicts in the Middle East, according to analysts.
State-owned Chinese firms have already invested hundreds of millions of US dollars to build the 4,750km (2,950 mile) Middle Corridor, which runs through Kazakhstan across the inland Caspian Sea by ship to Azerbaijan, onwards to Georgia and into Turkey.
China gave grant support of about US$70 million and equipment worth around US$2...

items.title: China’s first AI-powered cancer vaccine production line set to launch in Beijing
items.creator: Julie Zhang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s first AI-powered cancer vaccine production line set to launch in Beijing
items.content:
China has broken ground on what developers say is the country’s first production line for AI-assisted personalised tumour vaccines, raising hopes for millions of new cancer patients every year – a disease that ranks as the nation’s second-leading cause of death.
By October, Beijing-based Likang Life Sciences is expected to complete a new drug research and manufacturing centre in the Beijing Economic and Technological Development Zone, with a total investment of about 110 million yuan (US$16.1...

items.title: China adds 20 Japanese entities to export control list over remilitarisation concerns (原标题: China adds 20 Japanese entities to export-control list over remilitarisation concerns)
items.creator: Frank Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China adds 20 Japanese entities to export control list over remilitarisation concerns
items.content:
The Ministry of Commerce said on Monday that to deter Japan’s “remilitarisation” and its attempts to “acquire nuclear weapons”, it has added 20 Japanese entities “involved in enhancing Japan’s military capabilities” to an export-control list.
Twenty Japanese entities have also been added to a separate watch list.
The moves were made to safeguard China’s national security and interests and to fulfil international obligations such as non-proliferation, the ministry added.
A ministry spokesperson...

items.title: Economist Richard Koo on China and Japan’s shared ‘battle against time’
items.creator: Frank Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Economist Richard Koo on China and Japan’s shared ‘battle against time’
items.content:
Nomura Research Institute chief economist Richard Koo is well-known for his “balance sheet recession” interpretation of Japan’s economic downfall in the 1990s. In his second Open Questions interview, Koo assesses the risks the Chinese economy is facing this year and beyond, the many parallels between Beijing and Tokyo, and why market size and technology alone may not be enough to help avert a fate similar to Japan’s.
Koo also explains why giving China’s consumers money will not help and why...

items.title: China’s newest IP office to cut hi-tech patent approval times ‘by 70%’
items.creator: Sylvia Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s newest IP office to cut hi-tech patent approval times ‘by 70%’
items.content:
China has given the green light for a national intellectual property protection centre in a new megacity outside Beijing, a facility that is expected to cut approval times for hi-tech patents by about 70 per cent.
Once up and running, the centre in Xiongan New Area would provide expedited patent examination and one-stop intellectual property services for next-generation information technology and new materials, Xiongan authorities said in a statement on Saturday.
“[The centre] will help...

items.title: China’s big trucks go electric and abroad as subsidies help pave road to net-zero freight
items.creator: Daniel Ren
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s big trucks go electric and abroad as subsidies help pave road to net-zero freight
items.content:
Domestic makers of heavy-duty trucks are emerging as the latest beneficiaries of accelerating electrification on China’s roads, as technological gains and lower ownership costs are bolstering overseas sales amid a global energy crisis.
Southeast Asia and Africa, where Chinese makers have already established overseas assembly hubs, were expected to serve as new growth engines for companies ranging from FAW Jiefang to Foton Commercial Vehicles, according to analysts at S&P Global Ratings.
The two...

items.title: Beijing is building Shanghai into a major offshore yuan centre. Should Hong Kong worry?
items.creator: Sylvia Ma,Emma Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Beijing is building Shanghai into a major offshore yuan centre. Should Hong Kong worry?
items.content:
For the better part of two decades, Hong Kong has served as the undisputed poster child for Beijing’s ambitions to internationalise the yuan.
But in a world increasingly defined by sanctions, frozen assets and fears of US dollar weaponisation, policymakers are beginning to confront a strategic question: can a single offshore centre shoulder the burden of the yuan’s global ambitions?
That question lies at the heart of Beijing’s latest efforts to expand Shanghai’s offshore finance role, reviving a...

items.title: Why China’s tech firms could be in for a rude IPO surprise
items.creator: Jun Yan
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Why China’s tech firms could be in for a rude IPO surprise
items.content:
A listing path is not a valuation endorsement. China’s artificial intelligence (AI) and robotics companies are about to find out which parts of their private market premiums can survive public market pricing.
The Shanghai Stock Exchange has clarified how unprofitable AI large-model companies can apply under the Star Market’s fifth listing standard. The route is meant for companies with strategic technology that might not yet be reaping profits or substantial revenue.
A parallel test is taking...

items.title: China’s AI-powered laser mosquito zapper goes viral after crowdfunding success
items.creator: June Xia
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s AI-powered laser mosquito zapper goes viral after crowdfunding success
items.content:
A Chinese start-up that is developing an AI-powered laser mosquito zapper has raised US$2.7 million on the Indiegogo crowdfunding platform as it navigates the challenges of complex sensor calibrations and Western safety regulations.
Photon Matrix Lab, based in Changzhou, in eastern China’s Jiangsu province, has gone viral around the world with its portable laser mosquito defence system, which compresses industrial-grade lasers into a consumer device. Promising to rapidly detect and eliminate...

items.title: Global hydropower outlook report puts China at the head of the pack

items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Global hydropower outlook report puts China at the head of the pack
items.content:
China is cementing its dominance in global hydropower, highlighted by the Yarlung Tsangpo hydropower project – expected to be the world’s largest – and a massive pipeline of “pumped storage” facilities serving as Beijing’s strategic push to transform its fossil-fuel-reliant electricity grid into a stable, renewables-based network.
In its annual outlook report, the International Hydropower Association (IHA) said that with an estimated annual output capability of 300,000 gigawatt-hours, the...

items.title: Young Americans feel more threatened by AI than young Chinese. Why?
items.creator: Wei Wei
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Young Americans feel more threatened by AI than young Chinese. Why?
items.content:
My four-year-old son has become fascinated with his new friend, who has endless patience and an answer for everything. She is an artificial intelligence assistant on Doubao, one of China’s most popular AI applications.
My son, obsessed with space, black holes and galaxies, keeps asking Doubao for related videos. When the video is of low quality or inaccurate, I would stop it and explain it may not be reliable. Despite my concerns about AI-generated information, I let him interact with AI within...

items.title: In Venezuela, China’s oil-for-loan deals run into debt restructuring, US ‘gatekeeper’ risk
items.creator: Ji Siqi
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: In Venezuela, China’s oil-for-loan deals run into debt restructuring, US ‘gatekeeper’ risk
items.content:
The United States represents the primary obstacle to continuing oil-for-loan arrangements between China and Venezuela, analysts say, creating a significant wild card in what ranks among the largest debt restructurings in history.
Following Washington’s abduction in January of Venezuela’s then president, Nicolas Maduro, Caracas is set to reveal a US$240 billion debt pile, according to a Financial Times report on Wednesday.
The disclosure, expected in the coming weeks, rivals the €200 billion...

items.title: China Eastern buys 25 A330neo widebody jets as domestic interest rises in Airbus’ wings
items.creator: Xiaofei Xu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China Eastern buys 25 A330neo widebody jets as domestic interest rises in Airbus’ wings
items.content:
China Eastern Airlines has ordered 25 A330neo widebody jets from Airbus, marking the planemaker’s first major long-haul order from a Chinese carrier since last year, as the nation continues to favour the European manufacturer.
The list price of the 25 aircraft is about US$9.35 billion, but China Eastern said in a statement on Friday that it had obtained a “substantial price discount” from Airbus, making the actual cost significantly less than the catalogue price.
The Shanghai-based carrier...

items.title: EV influx, US rare earth miners blacklisted, China-EU trade talks
items.creator: Raymond Ma,Neil Denslow
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: EV influx, US rare earth miners blacklisted, China-EU trade talks
items.content:
Chinese automakers will debut more than 150 electric vehicles in the second half of the year, even as the industry contends with slowing domestic demand, profit-destroying price wars and international complaints about overcapacity.
BYD, Geely and other industry leaders will roll out multiple models costing about 100,000 yuan (US$14,700) during the next two months, according to HSBC, which compiled the data. That should support second-half demand in the entry-level and mass-market sectors, it...

items.title: China to generate 50% of electricity from nuclear and non-fossil sources by 2030
items.creator: Frank Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China to generate 50% of electricity from nuclear and non-fossil sources by 2030
items.content:
China is charting an ambitious five-year plan to harness nuclear and other non-fossil sources in a bid to generate half of the country’s electricity needs by 2030.
Recent oil shocks have strengthened Beijing’s resolve as it juggles decarbonising its energy sector and securing future demand, including that needed for artificial intelligence.
The plan, unveiled on Thursday by the National Energy Administration (NEA) and top economic planner National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC), also...

items.title: Indonesia set to issue US$1 billion in panda bonds in China
items.creator: Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Indonesia set to issue US$1 billion in panda bonds in China
items.content:
Indonesia is expected to start issuing at least US$1 billion in yuan-denominated bonds in China within days or weeks, the Southeast Asian country’s national news agency Antara said on Friday, as part of an effort to stabilise domestic finances and reduce dependence on the US dollar.
The Ministry of Finance in Jakarta said the planned issuance of panda bonds – debt securities sold in China’s domestic bond markets – could start before mid-July, pending final signoffs in Beijing, Antara...

items.title: As Chinese EVs expand global footprint, banks raise export forecasts
items.creator: Daniel Ren
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: As Chinese EVs expand global footprint, banks raise export forecasts
items.content:
Spurred by the rising popularity of electric vehicles (EVs), Chinese carmakers are consolidating their foothold in the global auto market, prompting international banks like Goldman Sachs to significantly raise their export forecasts.
Buoyant overseas shipments have also effectively created a cushion against falling domestic deliveries and an expected overall sales decline for Chinese automotive groups including BYD and Xpeng, analysts said.
“China’s new-energy vehicle exports have been...

items.title: At Chinese supply chain expo, executives look to tech as geopolitical insulation
items.creator: Ben Jiang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: At Chinese supply chain expo, executives look to tech as geopolitical insulation
items.content:
As wars, tariffs and geopolitical headwinds disrupt global commerce, industry experts at a trade fair in Beijing said technological advances and data collaboration could strengthen supply chain resilience.
At this week’s China International Supply Chain Expo, industry participants pointed to greater cross-border data coordination and China’s push into frontier technologies – including artificial intelligence and robotics – as critical moves that could build a more resilient global supply...
